jokes.text: He told me he doesn't want my help, so I'm just gonna play the supportive partner, and watch him fail.
jokes.setup: David being asked about Patrick's housewarming party
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive 'supportive' response of watching Patrick fail
jokes.explanation: Perfect David Rose character moment - passive aggressive disguised as supportive. The contradiction between 'supportive partner' and 'watch him fail' is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Remember that time that you told me you'd be there for me if I ever needed you? / I don't think that was me.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn trying to guilt Alexis with a past promise
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately denying she ever said that
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis - completely self-absorbed and honest about it. The immediate, casual denial is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: But unfortunately, the couple that massages us can only do it once a month, so...
jokes.setup: Roland explaining why they need a babysitter tonight
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and uncomfortable detail about their monthly couples massage
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland oversharing - the uncomfortable specificity about their massage schedule creates cringe humor.

jokes.text: Seriously. A nighttime couples massage, what is this, 1985?!
jokes.setup: David and Alexis reacting to Roland and Jocelyn's request
jokes.punchline: Alexis focusing on the wrong aspect - the timing rather than being excluded
jokes.explanation: Alexis completely missing the point that they were snubbed, instead critiquing the massage timing. Shows her self-absorption.

jokes.text: No! They asked everyone at the table except us, we're sitting right here!
jokes.setup: Alexis focusing on the wrong thing
jokes.punchline: David pointing out the actual slight they received
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between the siblings - David catches the real insult while Alexis is oblivious.

jokes.text: They're not going to ask someone of your age, John. / We raised two kids. / 'Raised' might be a stretch.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending their parenting credentials
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately undermining his claim with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - the casual way she deflates Johnny's parenting pride with air quotes around 'raised' is brilliant.

jokes.text: Our nursery was in a different wing of the house. / It was a design flaw. / I'm a light sleeper.
jokes.setup: Explaining their hands-off parenting
jokes.punchline: The escalating excuses revealing their complete detachment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how they justify their terrible parenting - from 'design flaw' to Moira's petty 'light sleeper' excuse.

jokes.text: I told you, it's a playpen, Moira. / I was hoping you were joking.
jokes.setup: Johnny working on baby furniture
jokes.punchline: Moira's horror at the reality of babysitting
jokes.explanation: Moira's deadpan delivery of hoping he was joking about the playpen shows her complete disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Roland and Jocelyn don't think we can take care of a kid. / That's an argument I'm willing to lose.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining why they need to prove themselves
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate surrender
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted Moira line - the formal phrasing of 'argument I'm willing to lose' for something so basic is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Remember? 'Not Without My Cousin.' / And Ronnie's TV gets that channel, I told you this, John! / Or at least I'm telling you now.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her movie with Joyce DeWitt
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous movie title and her admission she might not have mentioned it
jokes.explanation: The absurd movie title 'Not Without My Cousin' and Moira's casual admission she might not have mentioned it before is pure Moira.

jokes.text: Yeah, we were there most weeknights. / For at least a couple of hours. / Unless we were out for the evening. / Yeah, or jetting around. / Or if it was a really long day.
jokes.setup: Defending their parenting
jokes.punchline: The escalating list of excuses that negates their original claim
jokes.explanation: Beautiful escalating structure that completely undermines their initial claim of being present parents through increasingly ridiculous exceptions.

jokes.text: Besides, if Roland can look after a baby, anyone can.
jokes.setup: Johnny expressing confidence about babysitting
jokes.punchline: His logic being based on Roland being incompetent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's competitive nature and his opinion of Roland

jokes.text: and believe me, he will, he will have... the baby binder to hold his hand.
jokes.setup: Roland's mock confidence in Johnny
jokes.punchline: The condescending way he refers to the baby manual
jokes.explanation: Roland's patronizing tone about the 'baby binder holding his hand' shows his lack of faith in Johnny.

jokes.text: I've got him. I've got him, Jocelyn. / Well, I know why. It's because you don't trust the guy, and believe me, I don't either, but we're in this now, so...
jokes.setup: Jocelyn having trouble letting go of the baby
jokes.punchline: Roland openly admitting he doesn't trust Johnny while Johnny is right there
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland lack of filter - openly admitting distrust of Johnny right in front of him creates perfect cringe moment.

jokes.text: Looking so elegant for a night of babysitting. Just dressed to the nines in choking hazards.
jokes.setup: Roland commenting on Moira's outfit
jokes.punchline: The observation that her jewelry could be dangerous to a baby
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Moira's elaborate accessories being 'choking hazards' - good visual comedy and character awareness.

jokes.text: Oh wow, so it's just gonna be Johnny, alone with our precious miracle baby?
jokes.setup: Learning Moira won't be there
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's growing panic about leaving Johnny alone
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's growing horror at the reality of leaving Johnny alone with the baby builds the comedic tension.

jokes.text: Okay Johnny, just don't do anything I wouldn't do. / What?
jokes.setup: Roland giving parting advice
jokes.punchline: Johnny's confused reaction to this non-advice
jokes.explanation: Johnny's confused 'What?' to Roland's meaningless advice is a good reaction beat.

jokes.text: Fine, once. Twice. Fine, I had a tray in Cancun.
jokes.setup: Patrick being asked about party shots
jokes.punchline: His immediate escalation from denial to admitting he had a whole tray
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - Patrick's immediate backtracking from 'never' to 'a tray in Cancun' reveals his wild side.

jokes.text: Where is anybody gonna find glass-blown Venetian masks around here?
jokes.setup: Patrick dismissing David's party theme idea
jokes.punchline: David's oblivious question about finding luxury items in Schitt's Creek
jokes.explanation: Classic David - completely out of touch with their current reality, expecting luxury items in small-town Schitt's Creek.

jokes.text: Models and teen actors are just gonna lounge around in their bras and thongs, and take Polaroids of themselves all night?
jokes.setup: Patrick questioning David's idea of high school
jokes.punchline: The absurdly sexualized version of teenage life David apparently experienced
jokes.explanation: Patrick's incredulous description of David's privileged, sexualized high school experience contrasts perfectly with normal teenage life.

jokes.text: Besides, where are we going to find a salad bowl of E this last minute?
jokes.setup: David continuing to describe his party vision
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning needing a 'salad bowl of E' (ecstasy)
jokes.explanation: David's casual mention of needing drugs for a party, delivered as if it's a normal party supply, is darkly funny and very in character.

jokes.text: So forgive me for not wanting to run back into the fire.
jokes.setup: David explaining his therapy and past issues
jokes.punchline: Dramatic metaphor for attending a high school themed party
jokes.explanation: David's melodramatic 'run back into the fire' metaphor for a themed party shows his tendency to overdramatize.

jokes.text: My best friend, Summer, used to date Hef in high school, which isn't as weird as it sounds, 'cause he was like, a very young 77.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her Playboy Bunny costume choice
jokes.punchline: The impossible math and casual acceptance of an elderly man dating a teenager
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Alexis logic - 'very young 77' as if that makes it less weird. The absurdist math combined with her casual delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: Snip, snip, Mr. Mullens!
jokes.setup: Ted being drunk and making gestures
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate hand gesture reference to neutering
jokes.explanation: Drunk Ted making inappropriate gestures about his vet work - good character moment showing him loosening up.

jokes.text: You realize the bebe is crying. / I do, yes. / Isn't it scheduled to be dormant by now?
jokes.setup: Baby crying while they're getting ready to leave
jokes.punchline: Moira's expectation that babies follow a schedule like appliances
jokes.explanation: Moira's 'scheduled to be dormant' treats the baby like a machine - perfect character-specific humor about her detachment from reality.

jokes.text: Oh, it was the bebe. I'm so relieved!
jokes.setup: Johnny identifying the smell as a dirty diaper
jokes.punchline: Moira's relief that it's the baby and not something else
jokes.explanation: Moira's relief that the bad smell is 'just' the baby shows her skewed priorities and disconnect.

jokes.text: They're Tom Ford, John, no! And they wouldn't fit you anyway.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking for rubber gloves for the diaper
jokes.punchline: Moira protecting her designer gloves and adding insult about fit
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - protecting her Tom Ford gloves from baby duty and adding the unnecessary dig about them not fitting Johnny.

jokes.text: wipe counter-clockwise direction, ah... flip it, if you can flip it. / The baby?
jokes.setup: Moira reading diaper instructions
jokes.punchline: Johnny's horrified question about whether to flip the baby
jokes.explanation: The ambiguous instructions and Johnny's horrified clarification about flipping 'the baby' creates good absurdist humor.

jokes.text: It's not like the old days when we could just call Adelina, and have her rush home from her daughter's wedding.
jokes.setup: Moira worrying about Johnny handling the baby alone
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of making their nanny leave her daughter's wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of the Roses' former entitled lifestyle - casually mentioning making their nanny abandon her daughter's wedding.

jokes.text: when my cousin and I begin our backpack through Lebanon.
jokes.setup: Moira describing her movie plot
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous premise of cousins backpacking through Lebanon
jokes.explanation: The absurd movie plot continuation adds to the ridiculous premise established earlier.

jokes.text: never have I ever pretended to be a pizza delivery person so I could get into Jared Leto's Halloween party. / You're a bitch.
jokes.setup: David's never have I ever confession
jokes.punchline: Alexis's immediate hostile response to being called out
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - David's specific knowledge of Alexis's past and her immediate defensive response.

jokes.text: My mom was engaged to two pizza delivery guys. Can that count? 'Cause I am really thirsty.
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to participate in the drinking game
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation about her mom and practical motivation
jokes.explanation: Classic Stevie - deadpan delivery of personal information with practical motivation (wanting to drink).

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm just trying to think of something I haven't done.
jokes.setup: David struggling with the never have I ever game
jokes.punchline: His admission that he's done almost everything
jokes.explanation: David's struggle to find something he hasn't done reveals his wild past and current situation.

jokes.text: Because never have I ever been so bored.
jokes.setup: David asking how long the game will go on
jokes.punchline: Stevie's direct criticism of the game itself
jokes.explanation: Stevie's meta-commentary on the boring party game using the game's own format is cleverly done.

jokes.text: Eating out of the garbage? / Okay, I did that in front of you last night.
jokes.setup: Patrick suggesting something for never have I ever
jokes.punchline: David's immediate confession with specific recent example
jokes.explanation: David's immediate confession about eating garbage 'last night' shows how his standards have changed.

jokes.text: So I polished them off, and... it tasted like burnt plastic, and I regretted it instantly.
jokes.setup: Ted explaining he drank Alexis's coolers
jokes.punchline: His immediate regret and specific description of the taste
jokes.explanation: Ted's honest description of regretting the bad-tasting drinks shows his earnest personality.

jokes.text: I'm not sure if it's just the punch, but... he could get it!
jokes.setup: Twyla looking at Ted
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate drunken comment about Ted
jokes.explanation: Twyla's drunk confession creates uncomfortable moment, especially with Alexis right there.

jokes.text: Hey! Do you wanna put Emir away for a hot sec, so this game can keep going, and the party can end soon?
jokes.setup: David wanting to continue the bottle game
jokes.punchline: His honest admission he wants the party to end
jokes.explanation: David's brutally honest motivation - wanting the party to end - while trying to keep the game going is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm good. I don't wanna kiss anyone here. / My sister's sitting in the circle, You think I'm dying to play?
jokes.setup: David being asked to put his phone away
jokes.punchline: His point about not wanting to kiss his sister
jokes.explanation: David's logical objection to spin the bottle when his sister is playing shows practical thinking.

jokes.text: Babe, did you rig this? / Maybe I did, and maybe I did!
jokes.setup: Bottle landing on Ted and Alexis
jokes.punchline: Ted's drunk, enthusiastic non-denial
jokes.explanation: Drunk Ted's enthusiastic double confirmation about rigging the bottle is endearingly dorky.

jokes.text: Man, come here, big guy!
jokes.setup: Bottle pointing to David instead
jokes.punchline: Ted's aggressive enthusiasm to kiss David
jokes.explanation: Ted's drunk enthusiasm for kissing David creates awkward comedy, especially calling him 'big guy.'

jokes.text: Okay, I don't know where 'big guy' is coming from, but it needs to stop.
jokes.setup: Ted calling David 'big guy' repeatedly
jokes.punchline: David's formal complaint about the nickname
jokes.explanation: David's formal objection to Ted's casual nickname shows his discomfort with the whole situation.

jokes.text: if it weren't for Joyce Dewitt insisting on wearing those pigtails, this movie could've been a hit! We're supposed to be astrophysicists, it just takes you out of it.
jokes.setup: Moira critiquing her TV movie
jokes.punchline: Blaming the movie's failure on Joyce DeWitt's pigtails for astrophysicists
jokes.explanation: Moira's specific critique about pigtails ruining the astrophysicist credibility is absurdly detailed and very Moira.

jokes.text: It appears you may need a change yourself, Mr. Rose.
jokes.setup: Johnny covered in baby spit-up
jokes.punchline: Moira's formal observation about Johnny needing to change
jokes.explanation: Moira's formal way of pointing out Johnny needs to change clothes shows her detachment from the mess.

jokes.text: Its body is ejecting things, what did you do to it?!
jokes.setup: Baby spitting up everywhere
jokes.punchline: Moira treating the baby like a malfunctioning machine and blaming Johnny
jokes.explanation: Moira's 'its body is ejecting things' treats normal baby behavior like a mechanical malfunction - perfect character humor.

jokes.text: John, did you feed it shellfish?
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out why baby is spitting up
jokes.punchline: Moira's ridiculous suggestion about feeding shellfish to a baby
jokes.explanation: Moira's absurd suggestion about shellfish shows complete ignorance of baby care in a perfectly crafted way.

jokes.text: For all we know, it's got crawling pneumonia.
jokes.setup: Moira panicking about the baby's health
jokes.punchline: Her malapropism of 'crawling pneumonia' instead of 'walking pneumonia'
jokes.explanation: Moira's 'crawling pneumonia' malapropism is perfect for a baby-related context - shows her trying to sound medical but getting it wrong.

jokes.text: I don't want to say I told you so, but this is an infelicitous burden.
jokes.setup: Baby care going poorly
jokes.punchline: Moira's overly formal way of saying she was right while claiming not to gloat
jokes.explanation: Moira's 'infelicitous burden' is perfectly pretentious vocabulary for saying 'this sucks' - classic Moira character work.

jokes.text: Jocelyn's written, 'Spit happens.' / Very clever. / For Jocelyn.
jokes.setup: Reading advice in the baby binder
jokes.punchline: Moira's backhanded compliment about Jocelyn's pun
jokes.explanation: Moira's qualified praise 'for Jocelyn' perfectly captures her condescension while acknowledging the pun.

jokes.text: You remind me of me at a high school house party judging all the attractive and popular kids for being attractive and popular.
jokes.setup: Alexis observing David and Stevie being antisocial
jokes.punchline: Her self-aware analysis of judging popular kids while being judgmental herself
jokes.explanation: Alexis's meta-observation about judging people for being attractive while doing exactly that herself is surprisingly self-aware.

jokes.text: You're like a tsetse fly.
jokes.setup: Patrick saying he's having a great time
jokes.punchline: David's specific insult comparing Patrick to a disease-carrying fly
jokes.explanation: David's specific, somewhat sophisticated insult of 'tsetse fly' is perfectly pretentious and mean.

jokes.text: was it the time you kissed my boyfriend? Or... was it the time you kissed my boyfriend?
jokes.setup: Alexis confronting David about the kissing incident
jokes.punchline: Her repetitive emphasis making it clear there's only one incident but it's really bad
jokes.explanation: Alexis's repetitive structure emphasizes there's only one thing but it's really bothering her - good escalation.

jokes.text: what would you do if Patrick and I suddenly kissed at a party? / Or Patrick and me.
jokes.setup: Alexis challenging David hypothetically
jokes.punchline: Stevie immediately volunteering herself as another option
jokes.explanation: Stevie's immediate addition of herself as a kissing option adds to David's discomfort perfectly.

jokes.text: Okay, is that what needs to happen to clear the air here? You guys need to kiss?
jokes.setup: David being challenged about his jealousy
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overreaction suggesting they should kiss to prove his point
jokes.explanation: David's dramatic escalation to suggesting they kiss shows his jealous spiral getting out of hand.

jokes.text: Yum. That was fun.
jokes.setup: After kissing Patrick
jokes.punchline: Stevie's casual, positive assessment of kissing David's boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Stevie's deadpan 'Yum. That was fun.' after kissing Patrick is perfectly uncomfortable and characteristic.

jokes.text: It was really good for me.
jokes.setup: Everyone reacting to the kiss
jokes.punchline: Patrick's overly sexual assessment of the kiss
jokes.explanation: Patrick's 'really good for me' adds sexual innuendo that makes the situation more awkward.

jokes.text: Awful peaceful when he's sleeping.
jokes.setup: Looking at the sleeping baby after all the chaos
jokes.punchline: Johnny's relief at the quiet baby
jokes.explanation: Johnny's simple observation about the peaceful sleeping baby after the chaos is gentle humor.

jokes.text: That Adelina was grossly underpaid.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking what they learned about parenting
jokes.punchline: Moira's conclusion that their nanny was underpaid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira conclusion - instead of learning about parenting, she realizes they underpaid their help. Brilliant character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm even more convinced that our hands-off approach served them better.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on their parenting after babysitting
jokes.punchline: Moira doubling down on their neglectful parenting style
jokes.explanation: Moira learning the wrong lesson from babysitting - becoming more convinced of their hands-off approach - is perfect character work.

jokes.text: She had a real breakthrough during the denouement. I believe she believed we were cousins.
jokes.setup: Moira praising Joyce DeWitt's performance
jokes.punchline: Her assessment that DeWitt's best acting was believing they were really cousins
jokes.explanation: Moira's assessment that DeWitt's breakthrough was 'believing we were cousins' is absurdly specific acting critique.

jokes.text: Oh my gosh, you got little Roland Jr. to sleep? / Oh no, I'm talking about John.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn amazed someone got the baby to sleep
jokes.punchline: Moira clarifying she meant Johnny, not the baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke - the audience and Jocelyn assume she means the baby, but Moira means Johnny. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: He is a slice of meringue, Jocelyn.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn asking if everything went okay
jokes.punchline: Moira's bizarre food metaphor for describing the baby
jokes.explanation: 'Slice of meringue' is such a wonderfully specific and weird Moira metaphor for a sweet, light baby - pure character gold.

jokes.text: I can actually see myself leaving Roland Jr. With you people more often. / Well, that something Linda might want to massage right out of your mind, the next appointment.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn suggesting they babysit more often
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate suggestion to have that idea massaged away
jokes.explanation: Moira's suggestion that Jocelyn get the babysitting idea 'massaged out' of her mind is perfectly petty and clever.

jokes.text: You know, I almost think I like him better asleep than awake.
jokes.setup: Roland looking at his sleeping son
jokes.punchline: His honest admission about preferring his baby unconscious
jokes.explanation: Roland's honest parenting confession about preferring his baby asleep is darkly relatable humor.

jokes.text: We're just gonna go for a ride, see where it takes us. Maybe start a vet clinic somewhere along the way. He'll the animals back to heath, I'll... offer grooming services.
jokes.setup: Alexis threatening to run away with Ted
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate fantasy about starting a vet clinic with grooming services
jokes.explanation: Alexis's romantic runaway fantasy immediately becoming a practical business plan is perfectly her mix of dramatic and shallow.

jokes.text: So, you think he's handsome then? / What? He's like-he goes to the gym.
jokes.setup: David probing Patrick's jealousy
jokes.punchline: Patrick's flustered admission about Ted's physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Patrick's flustered response about Ted going to the gym reveals his attraction while trying to deflect.

jokes.text: Both?
jokes.setup: David asking if Patrick was jealous of him or Ted
jokes.punchline: Patrick's honest admission that he was jealous of both
jokes.explanation: Patrick's one-word answer 'Both?' is perfectly crafted - maximum impact with minimal words, revealing his confused attraction.

jokes.text: Well, unless you were into candy ravers with asymmetrical haircuts and a lot of pacifier necklaces, I think our paths crossed at the right time.
jokes.setup: Patrick wondering about meeting in high school
jokes.punchline: David's specific description of his high school aesthetic
jokes.explanation: David's specific details about his rave phase with 'pacifier necklaces' perfectly captures his wild past with characteristic specificity.

jokes.text: That was fun. / Yes / That was really fun. Did you have fun? / Yes, Ted. / Did everyone had fun? / Yes, Ted.
jokes.setup: Ted being overly enthusiastic after the party
jokes.punchline: His repetitive need for confirmation that everyone had fun
jokes.explanation: Drunk Ted's anxious need for validation that everyone had fun shows his earnest people-pleasing nature.

jokes.text: You kissed my brother tonight, I'm not kissing you after you kissed my brother. / Pffft! Who kissed your brother?
jokes.setup: Alexis refusing to kiss Ted
jokes.punchline: Ted's drunk confusion about who he kissed
jokes.explanation: Ted's drunk confusion about kissing David shows how wasted he is and creates good callback humor.

jokes.text: But first just give your brother a kiss. / Ew, Ted, Ew!
jokes.setup: Ted still being inappropriately drunk
jokes.punchline: His suggestion that Alexis kiss her own brother
jokes.explanation: Ted's drunk suggestion gets appropriately disgusted reaction - shows how far gone he is.
